headline: Mexico opens the World Cup at the Azteca — and Ochoa's sixth
summary: Mexico open WC 2026 against South Africa at Estadio Azteca on June 11, making the Azteca the first stadium ever to host three World Cup opening matches (1970, 1986, 2026). The fixture revives the 2010 opener between the same nations. Veteran goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa, 40, is in line to join Messi and Ronaldo as one of the only men to appear at six World Cups — and could be among the first to take the field at a sixth.
